title: L’exploration du monde — Exploring our
subject: French
description:
world
In Unit 2, students move beyond their personal world to how they engage with the wider world.
They do this by exploring options for personal travel and exploration in French-speaking
communities and Australia, and by considering the associated cultural conventions. Students
consider the ways that French culture has contributed to the world, and reflect upon their
experiences, compare options and express preferences, while appreciating diverse cultural
values.
Unit objectives
1. Comprehend French to understand information, ideas, opinions and experiences related to
travel and exploration, social customs and French influences around the world.
2. Identify tone, purpose, context and audience to infer meaning related to travel and
exploration, social customs and French influences around the world.
3. Analyse and evaluate information and ideas to draw conclusions related to travel and
exploration, social customs and French influences around the world.
4. Apply knowledge of language elements of French to construct meaning related to travel and
exploration, social customs and French influences around the world.
5. Structure, sequence and synthesise information to justify opinions and perspectives related
to travel and exploration, social customs and French influences around the world.
6. Communicate using contextually appropriate French related to travel and exploration, social
customs and French influences around the world.
French 2025 v1.3 Units
